title: Validated pre/post misconception instrument (items mapped to the inventory)
context: Measuring misconception reduction in real learners is the project's primary success metric — not page views or publication. This task creates the pre/post instrument that measures whether learners' understanding of cancer statistics actually improves after completing a course. The instrument must map one-to-one to the named misconception inventory, including the 'survival-rise implies screening benefit' and base-rate items. It may adapt a validated risk-literacy instrument (per its license, verified before use) or build and lightly validate a bespoke instrument. Stats-reviewer sign-off on item correctness required.
objective: Create a validated pre/post misconception measurement instrument (survey/quiz) with items that map one-to-one to the named misconception inventory from oncology-data-literacy-curriculum-002. The instrument must include items covering at minimum: (1) the survival-rise implies screening/treatment benefit misconception; (2) the base-rate fallacy for test accuracy; (3) crude vs age-standardized rates confusion; (4) relative vs absolute risk confusion; (5) lifetime risk as personal probability error. Scoring and interpretation must be documented.
acceptanceCriteria: Pre/post items map one-to-one to the misconception inventory, including the 'survival-rise implies screening/treatment saves lives' item and the base-rate fallacy item
Either adapts a validated risk-literacy instrument (with verified license permitting adaptation) or builds a bespoke instrument that has been lightly validated on a small pilot group
Scoring methodology and interpretation documented (e.g., how to compute misconception reduction, what constitutes meaningful pre-to-post improvement)
Items are reviewed by the stats reviewer for statistical correctness of every stem and correct-answer key
Instrument is structured as an anonymous, opt-in assessment; no personally identifying data is collected or required
Biostatistician/epidemiologist sign-off on item correctness recorded in the reviewers ledger
output: A validated pre/post misconception assessment instrument with items mapped to the full misconception inventory, documented scoring methodology, and stats-reviewer sign-off recorded — the measurement tool that determines whether the courses achieve their core objective.
